title: designer path placement
description: A short quiz on the core ideas of the Designer path. Answer what you know. Each chapter you place out of is one you can skip ahead on, and you can revisit any chapter any time.

scoring.chapterSkipRule: Each chapter is tested by 2 questions worth 2 points each. A learner places out of a chapter (skips its Version A) by answering both of its questions correctly. Overall, 50 percent or more of maxScore recommends Version B as the default for chapters not individually skipped.

questions.prompt: You want a small tool that takes a product name and returns a styled caption, every time, without you opening a chat. What makes that possible?

options.label: Calling an AI model through its API from code, so the tool runs the model itself
Nothing, AI only works inside a chat window
Saving a chat conversation as a file
Screenshotting the chat each time

questions.prompt: Calling an AI model through its API many times has a cost. What is the right mindset?

options.label: Each call costs a small amount, so at scale you plan and watch the total
API calls are always free
The cost is the same whether you call it once or a million times
Cost only applies to image tools, not text

questions.prompt: Which image prompt is more likely to give you something usable?

options.label: One naming the subject, style, mood, colors, and what to leave out
"make a nice image"
The shortest prompt possible
It does not matter, the tool decides anyway

questions.prompt: The image came out close but the lighting is wrong. What is the best next move?

options.label: Adjust the prompt to describe the lighting you want, and generate again
Run the exact same prompt and hope it changes
Accept it, you only get one try per prompt
Switch to a completely different subject

questions.prompt: You need ten variations of a hero image to test. Which approach fits AI image generation best?

options.label: Generate a batch from one prompt, varying it slightly per run, then pick the best
Generate one image and manually edit it ten times
Ask a text chatbot to write ten descriptions
It cannot be done, image tools make one image ever

questions.prompt: Out of a generated batch, some images are off-brand. What is the realistic workflow?

options.label: Generate, then review and filter, expecting to cut or redo the misses
Use every image, the tool never makes off-brand work
Decide AI image tools are useless
Never look at the output, just ship it

questions.prompt: You have one approved layout and need it produced in 50 different sizes and language versions. What is the efficient approach?

options.label: Programmatic design: code generates all 50 variants from the one layout and a list of inputs
Resize and retype all 50 by hand in a design app
Pick five and hope nobody needs the rest
Ask a chatbot to describe each of the 50

questions.prompt: Programmatic design is the strongest fit for which kind of work?

options.label: Repetitive, rule-based variations of a design across many inputs
A single one-off illustration that needs an artist's hand
Deciding the brand's overall creative direction
Nothing, design can never be done with code
